lesson.menu_desc: Help a friend with a problem using should and shouldn't -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Giving Advice -- helping a friend with should and shouldn't
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 17 -- Giving Advice

listenings.text: Hi Carolina, it's Bruno. I'm sorry to call so late, but I really need your advice. I feel so stressed right now. I have too much homework this week, and there is a big test on Monday. I am really worried about my grades. The worst part is at night: I can't sleep, because I keep thinking about everything I have to do. I tried to study all night, but it didn't help, and now I am even more tired. I don't know what I should do first. Should I study math or English? Should I ask the teacher for more time? Please, when you get this message, call me back and tell me what you think I should do. I trust your advice. Thanks, my friend. Talk soon. Bye.

Good morning, students. Exam week is coming, and I know many of you feel stressed and worried. So today I want to give you some simple advice. First, you should study a little every day, not everything in one night. Your brain learns better that way. Second, you should sleep well, because sleep helps your memory. Third, you should take short breaks to stay calm. Now, here are two things you shouldn't do. You shouldn't leave everything for the last day, and you shouldn't compare yourself to other students. Everyone is different. Remember: you work hard every day, so you deserve to feel calm and proud of your effort. If you feel too worried, please come and talk to me. You are not alone. Good luck, everyone. You've got this.

extra_audio.text: Carolina is giving advice to her friend Bruno. First, Bruno tells Carolina he is stressed and asks her for advice. Then, she says he should make a study plan and focus on one subject. After that, she says he shouldn't study all night, because he needs to sleep. Finally, she suggests a short break and reminds him he deserves to feel calm.
